name: Fischer Esterification
mechanism_summary: Protonation of the carbonyl oxygen by H+ increases its electrophilicity. The alcohol oxygen attacks nucleophorically, followed by proton transfers and loss of water to yield the ester.

name: Amide Bond Formation
mechanism_summary: Nucleophilic attack of the amine on the activated carboxylic acid carbonyl, followed by elimination of a leaving group (usually water or equivalent).

name: Alcohol Oxidation (Primary to Acid)
mechanism_summary: Initial formation of a chromate ester followed by elimination to form an aldehyde, which is then hydrated and further oxidized to the carboxylic acid.

name: Grignard Addition to Ketone
mechanism_summary: The carbanionic character of the Grignard reagent attacks the electrophilic carbonyl carbon. The resulting alkoxide is then protonated during acidic workup to form a tertiary alcohol.
